objective: Add an upstream-compatible Codex CLI provider to Antigravity-Manager and assess the value of a separate headless Codex MCP companion.
edits: Added `proxy.codex` config types/defaults in Rust and TypeScript.
Added `src-tauri/src/proxy/providers/codex_cli.rs` with a Codex app-server runtime manager, JSON-RPC worker pool, model discovery, request shaping, and response helpers.
Wired Codex runtime state through proxy service state, Axum server state, save-config hot updates, and new Tauri/admin commands.
Hooked OpenAI handlers to route `codex:*` model targets through the Codex provider for `/v1/chat/completions`, `/v1/responses`, and `/v1/completions` compatibility paths.
Extended `/v1/models` and the API Proxy UI with Codex provider diagnostics, controls, and router-target copy helpers.
commands: python inline probes against `codex.cmd app-server` for initialize/model/list/thread/start/turn/start

passed: Confirmed Codex app-server uses newline-delimited JSON-RPC over stdio and supports initialize, getAuthStatus, model/list, thread/start, and turn/start.
Confirmed `git diff --check` passed with no whitespace/conflict-marker issues.

failed_or_blocked.reason: `tsc` is not available because project frontend dependencies are not installed locally (`node_modules/.bin/tsc` missing).
TypeScript compiler is not installed in the project; npm attempted to fall back to the unrelated `tsc` package.
Rust toolchain is not installed or not on PATH on this machine (`cargo` and `rustc` are unavailable).

notes: The Codex provider currently treats OpenAI payloads as a compatibility layer by flattening them into prompt text plus optional image inputs for ephemeral Codex app-server turns.
Client-supplied tool schemas are rejected clearly for Codex-backed requests because the Codex app-server protocol is not a direct OpenAI Responses tool-schema passthrough.
